description: From shoulder to hand, 'pelio' is the upper limb. It reaches and carries, and it can draw someone close. 'manuwe' (hand) is the grasping part at its end, while 'talua' (shoulder) is the joint where it meets the torso.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release them into middle-height front unrounded 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and continue into stressed high-front unrounded 'i'. Keep final middle-height back rounded 'o' in its own syllable across the hiatus.
usage_notes: 'pelio' covers only the body part. An English source using 'arm' for a weapon or for equipping with weapons keeps that separate sense explicit.

semantic_domains.physical: an upper limb used for reach, support, and bodily contact
